pkg/expand.purpose: Generic type-safe expand/field-selection mechanism for API responses. Callers declare an Expandable[T] string-enum type; Expand[T] is a validated slice of those values with Has/With/Without/SetOrUnsetIf helpers used in service input types to control which nested Ent relations are eager-loaded.

patterns.name: Expandable enum type
patterns.description: Define a string-based type implementing Expandable[T] with Values() []T listing all valid keys; use as the type parameter for Expand[T] in service input structs.

patterns.name: Expand.Validate() in service input
patterns.description: Call input.Expand.Validate() inside the input type's Validate() so unknown expand values are rejected before the adapter.
patterns.example: if err := input.Expand.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: Expand.Has() in adapter Ent queries
patterns.description: Use expand.Has(SomeValue) to conditionally append Ent WithX() eager-load calls.
patterns.example:
q := client.Invoice.Query()
if params.Expand.Has(InvoiceExpandLines) { q = q.WithLines() }

patterns.name: SetOrUnsetIf for conditional assembly
patterns.description: Build expand slices from boolean flags via SetOrUnsetIf(condition, value) rather than manual append/filter.

key_file_guides.role: Complete package API: Expandable interface, Expand[T] type, and all methods (Validate, Has, Clone, With, Without, SetOrUnsetIf).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Expandable requires comparable — string enums satisfy this, struct keys do not compile; Values() returning empty slice makes Validate() reject all expand values.

anti_patterns: Passing raw string slices for expand fields instead of typed Expand[T] — loses compile-time safety and Validate().
Skipping Expand.Validate() in input validation — unknown expand values silently no-op in adapter queries.
Implementing Values() to return an empty slice — Validate() rejects all expand values including valid ones.
Constructing Expand[T] with non-comparable type parameters — will not compile.

decisions.decision: Expand is a separate generic package rather than inlined per-domain.
decisions.rationale: Billing, subscription, and customer need the same validated expand-slice semantics; a shared package centralizes Has/With/Without and the single-operator validation.

code_examples.scenario: Define and use a typed expand for invoice queries

pkg/featuregate.purpose: Minimal namespace-scoped boolean feature-gate abstraction. Defines the Gate interface (EvaluateBool) and a Noop implementation that always returns true, allowing consumers to depend on the interface while gating logic is supplied elsewhere or disabled.

patterns.name: Gate interface for all evaluations
patterns.description: Consumers depend on the Gate interface and call EvaluateBool(namespace, flag, defaultValue) — never inspect a concrete gate type.

patterns.name: Noop returns true (allow) by default
patterns.description: NewNoop() returns a Noop whose EvaluateBool always returns (true, nil), enabling all gated paths when no real gate is wired.

key_file_guides.role: Sole file: Gate interface, Noop struct, NewNoop() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Noop.EvaluateBool ignores the defaultValue and always returns true — relying on the default param against a Noop gate is a no-op.

anti_patterns: Type-asserting to the concrete Noop type instead of depending on the Gate interface.
Assuming EvaluateBool honors defaultValue under Noop — Noop always returns true.

decisions.rationale: Widely imported (server, app/common, billing worker, productcatalog); a permissive default lets features run when no gating backend is configured.

code_examples.scenario: Depend on the Gate interface and evaluate a flag

pkg/ffx.purpose: Minimal feature-flag service with three implementations (static, context-based, test hybrid). All flag reads go through the Service interface so callers are decoupled from the backing store; the primary constraint is that unknown features return an error, not false.

patterns.name: Service interface for all reads
patterns.description: All consumers call Service.IsFeatureEnabled(ctx, Feature) — never inspect AccessConfig directly outside this package.
patterns.example:
svc := ffx.NewStaticService(ffx.AccessConfig{MyFeature: true})
enabled, err := svc.IsFeatureEnabled(ctx, MyFeature)

patterns.name: Named Feature constants, not raw strings
patterns.description: Feature is a string type; declare named constants and pass them to IsFeatureEnabled. Raw literals bypass static reference checking.

patterns.name: Context-carried AccessConfig for per-request flags
patterns.description: SetAccessOnContext injects an AccessConfig into ctx; NewContextService reads it back. Missing key returns ErrContextMissing, not false.

patterns.name: Test hybrid service falls back to static
patterns.description: NewTestContextService tries ctx first then provided static defaults, letting tests override individual flags without rebuilding context.

key_file_guides.role: Declares Feature, AccessConfig, and the Service interface — single source of truth for the contract.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AccessConfig.Merge mutates the receiver — pass a fresh copy if the original must stay unchanged.
contextService errors if the feature key is absent; callers must handle the error, not just the bool.

key_file_guides.role: staticService backed by an in-process AccessConfig map; used for startup wiring and test defaults.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns error (not false) for unknown features — do not assume unknown == disabled.

anti_patterns: Bypassing Service by reading AccessConfig maps directly outside this package.
Using raw string literals instead of typed Feature constants.
Ignoring the error return from IsFeatureEnabled — unknown feature != false.
Storing AccessConfig in a global variable instead of context or a Wire-injected service.

decisions.decision: Three separate service implementations (static, context, test hybrid).
decisions.rationale: Static is safe at startup; context serves per-request tenant flags; the test hybrid overrides without rebuilding context — all without nil checks or type switches in callers.

code_examples.scenario: Wire a static service and check a flag in a handler

pkg/filter.purpose: Typed, composable query filters (string, integer, float, time, boolean, ULID) that compile to both Ent selector predicates (func(*sql.Selector)) and go-sqlbuilder WHERE expressions (string). Every filter type implements the Filter interface and enforces single-operator-per-node validation.

patterns.name: Dual output — Ent selector and go-sqlbuilder expression
patterns.description: Every filter implements Select(field) func(*sql.Selector) for Ent/Postgres and SelectWhereExpr(field, q) string for ClickHouse; new types must implement both.

patterns.name: Single-operator validation
patterns.description: validateSingleOperator(f) uses reflection to count non-nil fields; public Validate() wraps with models.NewNillableGenericValidationError exactly once — never double-wrap.
patterns.example: if err := f.Validate(); err != nil { /* err is models.GenericValidationError */ }

patterns.name: Recursive And/Or with depth limit
patterns.description: Each type carries *[]FilterT And and Or fields; ValidateWithComplexity(maxDepth) recurses and returns ErrFilterComplexityExceeded when maxDepth <= 0.

patterns.name: IsEmpty / nil guard before applying predicate
patterns.description: Select returns nil for empty filters — check IsEmpty() or nil before applying to an Ent selector.
patterns.example: if p := f.Select("field"); p != nil { query.Where(p) }

patterns.name: Contains uses ILIKE with auto-escaped metacharacters
patterns.description: $contains/$ncontains wrap the value with EscapeLikePattern + ContainsPattern before ILIKE; raw $like/$ilike pass values unescaped.

key_file_guides.role: All filter struct types, the Filter interface, LIKE-safety helpers (EscapeLikePattern, ContainsPattern, ReverseContainsPattern).
key_file_guides.watch_for: $like is caller-controlled, $contains is auto-escaped — do not pass an already-percent-wrapped value to $contains; Match() returns ErrOperationNotSupported for Like/Nlike/Ilike/Nilike (SQL-only).

key_file_guides.role: Dual-path tests validating SelectWhereExpr and Select branches for every operator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SQL placeholder syntax differs (? vs $1); use newSelectBuilder() for Ent, sqlbuilder.Select for go-sqlbuilder.

anti_patterns: Setting multiple operator fields on the same node — Validate returns ErrFilterMultipleOperators.
Applying Select() without a nil check — empty filter returns nil and panics on the selector.
Passing pre-escaped LIKE patterns to $contains/$ncontains — double-escaping.
Adding a new filter type without both Select and SelectWhereExpr.
Wrapping Validate() result in a second GenericValidationError — public Validate() wraps once.

decisions.decision: Dual output (Ent selector + go-sqlbuilder expression) on a single Filter type.
decisions.rationale: Adapters use Ent for Postgres and go-sqlbuilder for ClickHouse; one filter struct drives both backends without separate translation layers.

decisions.decision: Validation wraps in GenericValidationError exactly once at the public boundary.
decisions.rationale: Services forward Validate() results to HTTP encoders mapping GenericValidationError to 400; double-wrapping breaks errors.Is checks.

code_examples.scenario: Apply a validated FilterString to both Ent and ClickHouse queries

pkg/gosundheit.purpose: Thin adapter bridging the go-sundheit health-check library to OpenMeter's structured slog logger. Implements health.CheckListener so health-check lifecycle events emit structured log lines at Warn on error and Debug on success.

patterns.name: Return health.CheckListener interface from constructor
patterns.description: checkListener is unexported; consumers receive a health.CheckListener from NewLogger — do not expose the concrete struct.

patterns.name: Warn on error, Debug on success
patterns.description: OnCheckRegistered and OnCheckCompleted log at Warn when result.Error != nil and Debug otherwise; keep this split for any new listener method.
patterns.example:
if result.Error != nil { c.logger.Warn("health check failed", slog.String("check", name), slog.Any("error", result.Error)); return }
c.logger.Debug("health check completed", slog.String("check", name))

key_file_guides.role: Sole file: unexported checkListener + exported NewLogger; implements only health.CheckListener.
key_file_guides.watch_for: go-sundheit's CheckListener may add methods in future versions — this file must be updated or builds break.

anti_patterns: Logging at Error level for health-check failures — Warn is intentional; the library handles alerting.
Storing state in checkListener — it is a pure pass-through to the logger.
Exposing checkListener as a concrete type outside the package.

decisions.decision: Package wraps go-sundheit's CheckListener rather than using it directly in startup code.
decisions.rationale: Centralizes log severity and field naming for health events; app/common wires it once so all binaries get uniform health-check logging.
